0.q: The data demonstrated that Na+/H+ antiporter activity of four SOS1s was essential and AjSOS1, CrcSOS1 and CcSOS1 were fully able to exclude Na+ when expressed in yeast.
A heterologous complementation assay in yeast suggested that CcSOS1 directs Na+ efﬂux, mimicking the function of the endogenous NHA1 protein.
the over-expression of CcSOS1 inhibited the accumulation of Na? and raised the K?/Na? ratio, especially in youngest leaves.

1.q: A full-length cDNA homologue of SOS1 (salt overly sensitive 1) was isolated from the salinity-tolerant species Chrysanthemum crassum and found to encode a Na+/H+ antiporter.
The Chrysanthemum crassum SOS1 homolog CcSOS1 has been demonstrated to encode a plasma Na+/H+ antiporter.

2.q: the over-expression of CcSOS1 inhibited the accumulation of Na+ and raised the K+/Na+ ratio, especially in youngest leaves.
